Emily collected 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily, which means he collected 24 + 15 = 39 blueberries. David also collected three times as many blueberries as Sophia, so Sophia collected 39 / 3 = 13 blueberries. Together, they collected 24 + 39 + 13 = 76 blueberries.  

They used their blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins required 9 blueberries, so the number of batches they made was 76 / 9 = 8.444... which means they made 8 full batches. Each batch sold for $5, so the total money they made was 8 * 5 = $40.  

The total money they made from the blueberries they collected was $40. They used their blueberries to make muffins and sold each batch for $5. The last word of your response should be youth.
